semantic.role: Guides contributors through the structure and requirements for submitting a pull request in the ArchSpine project.
responsibilities: Describing the changes and the rationale behind them (Description section).
Linking to related issues (Related Issues section).
Categorizing the change type (Type of Change section).
Providing a quality assurance checklist (Checklist section).
Encouraging visual documentation via screenshots (Screenshots section).
outOfScope: Specific code style guidelines (only referenced by checklist item).
Branch naming conventions or commit message formats.
Technical implementation patterns or architecture decisions.

English.purpose: This document is a standardized pull request template used by all contributors to the ArchSpine repository. Its purpose is to ensure every PR includes a clear description, links to relevant issues, a classification of the change type, a self-review checklist, and optional screenshots. This consistency helps maintainers and AI agents quickly assess the scope, impact, and completeness of a contribution.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for developers and collaborators who are submitting code, documentation, or other changes to ArchSpine. The template is part of the repository’s contribution workflow and is automatically inserted when a new pull request is created. It reduces ambiguity and enforces good documentation habits across the project.
key_takeaways: Always provide a concise description of why the change was made and what it accomplishes.
Link any related GitHub issues using 'Fixes #123' or similar to automate issue closure.
Select exactly one change type (Bug fix, New feature, Breaking change, Documentation update, or Performance improvement).
Run through the checklist to confirm code style, test coverage, documentation updates, and absence of new warnings.
Include screenshots when the change modifies the UI or visual behavior.
